dialogos.texto_en: Uwaaa...
This is...kind of unbelievable...
Yeah, it's completely immersed in there.
Oh, if it isn't Miku and Raiha?\nWhat are you looking at?
Ah, Futaro and Itsuki.\nThis.
Eh? A snake in a bottle?\nWhat is this?
Could this be habu sake?
Yes, it's habu sake.
It's entirely submerged in the sake.\nIt might be scary for those \nwho are afraid of snakes.
Even if you're not afraid,\nthe impact is incredible.\nIt feels like it's still alive.
Yes, it'd take a lot of courage to drink this.\nI wonder what it tastes like.
You're underage, so don't drink it.
I-I know that without you telling me!
The first person who put a poisonous snake in alcohol\nmust have been courageous.
Even so, there are surprisingly many foods like that.
Like fugu liver?\nThere are also some foods that take years\nto remove the toxins to even become edible.
Do you know?\nThey still don't know the reason why the poison disappears.
Oh, really?\nSo you eat it because it's delicious\neven though you don't know why?
That's right.
I bow my head to the human desire for\nunending culinary exploration!
I wonder if this habu sake is actually tasty?
I don't know.\nWe'll have to wait a while to know the taste.
Habu...\nCome to think of it, mongooses and\nhabu snakes are enemies, right?
Oh, yes, I've heard that.\nThey're supposed to be natural enemies.
No, that perception is wrong.
Really?
Originally, the mongoose was brought to Okinawa\nas an animal to eradicate the habu%p-2;――
But it didn't seem to be of much help.
Why is that?\nDidn't they fight?
There were plenty of other animals to feed on\nwithout having to risk dangerous battles\nwith habu snakes.
So the mongooses ended up eating a lot of\nother animals without attacking the habu,\nwhich resulted in the destruction of the ecosystem.
I see...
Actually, the number of indigenous creatures\nin Okinawa has decreased due to the impact\nof the mongoose. Have you heard of the Yanbaru rail?
Basically, the desire for them to be natural enemies\nis just a human wish.\nThey're not really enemies.
These animals were just tossed around by human desires.
I didn't know that.\nI always thought they were like dogs and cats.
Proverbs are overused\nand turn into a fixed way of thinking.
...Heheh.
What's wrong?
Oh, nothing.\nIt just reminded me of something from the past.
From the past?
The time when I first saw you two interact,\nFutaro, Itsuki.\nYou two were like cats and dogs back then.
Ah...
...Well, there was a time like that, huh.
And now, you two are lovers. Wow.
Don't say that...\nIt's embarrassing.
Itsuki, you were so cold and distant before.
But I couldn't help it back then!\nI still didn't understand much about Uesugi-kun.
Plus, the past is the past, right, Uesugi-ku...\nFutaro?!
That's right. Regardless of the past,\nit's already something that's already happened.
Well then, we'll leave it at that.\nYou two lovebirds can spend time together.
People can change.\nI've really felt that since I met you guys.
We feel the same way.\nWe've changed too, since we met you.
That's right. Especially Itsuki.
For sure, if I were to tell my old self\nabout the present, she wouldn't believe me.
That, well...\nI'm dating Futaro.
I'm sure me, nor anyone else, would have believed it.
Well then, we'll leave it at that.\nYou two lovebirds can spend time together.
I could never forget that encounter.\nIt was THAT impactful.
Yes, definitely.\nHow you could say something like that to a complete stranger.
Big bro, what did you say?
I can kind of imagine it though.
You don't need to picture that!
However, looking back on it now,\nthat meeting was also a good memory.
Absolutely.
Uesu...\nFutaro, you think so too, right?
I mean, if it wasn't for that encounter,\nwe wouldn't be where we are now.
Well then, we'll leave it at that.\nYou two lovebirds can spend time together.
For sure.\nMake sure to escort Itsuki-san properly, big bro.
Ah, Miku?!\nYou don't have to worry so much%p-2;――
Ah...... They left...
Well, I guess we'll just have to take their word for it.
Y-yeah.
